$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Approval Envelope, Step, and Compensation Step rules. The root schema describes the common envelope representation. The approval_envelope_submission and stored_approval_envelope definitions below define the POST request profile and Registry-stored/read profile. The creator_signature is an EdDSA signature over the JCS-canonical submission with creator_signature set to empty string and creator_kid included. The principal_signature covers the immutable submitted content plus principal_kid and principal_signature set to empty string, excluding Registry-managed lifecycle fields.
title: AIP Approval Envelope
description: A Chained Approval Envelope. ApprovalEnvelopeSubmission is submitted to POST /v1/approvals; StoredApprovalEnvelope is returned by Registry read and mutation endpoints with Registry-managed lifecycle fields. Allows a principal to pre-approve a complete multi-step workflow graph in a single signing ceremony.

approval_id.description: Immutable unique identifier for this Approval Envelope. Generated by the orchestrating agent before submission. Format: 'apr:' prefix followed by a UUID v4 in canonical lowercase hyphenated form.

created_by.description: AID of the orchestrating agent that constructed and submitted this envelope. MUST be a registered AIP agent.

creator_kid.description: DID URL identifying the Ed25519 verification method used to verify creator_signature. The DID portion MUST equal created_by.

principal_id.description: W3C DID of the human or organisational principal who must approve this envelope. MUST NOT use the did:aip method — principals are humans or organisations, never AIP agents.

engagement_id.description: OPTIONAL. Engagement Object identifier this Approval Envelope is scoped to. When present, this field is part of the signed Approval Envelope body and MUST be included in creator_signature and principal_signature canonical signing inputs.

description.description: Plain-language description of the complete workflow. Displayed verbatim to the principal during the approval ceremony. MUST be human-readable and accurately represent all steps.

approval_window_expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime after which the envelope transitions to 'expired' if not approved. MUST be strictly after created_at and MUST NOT be more than 72 hours after created_at. Registries reject overlong windows with approval_envelope_invalid. Once expired, no approval is possible and the envelope MUST NOT be re-submitted. This field is an approval deadline only; once approved before the deadline, later passage of this timestamp MUST NOT block step claims or execution.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime when this envelope was constructed by the orchestrating agent. MUST NOT be in the future beyond 30-second clock skew at Registry submission time.

steps.description: Ordered array of Step objects representing the forward execution DAG. step_index values MUST be unique and sequential (1-based). The DAG defined by triggered_by MUST be acyclic — circular dependencies MUST be rejected with approval_envelope_invalid.

compensation_steps.description: OPTIONAL. Array of Compensation Step objects for SAGA rollback. Referenced by steps[].compensation_index (1-based identifier, not a zero-based array offset). When approved, both forward steps and compensation steps receive the principal's consent. A compensation step is selected only through a forward step's compensation_index; the Registry MUST NOT infer relationships from action_type, actor, relying_party_uri, or array position.

total_value.description: OPTIONAL. Total maximum financial exposure of this workflow. When present, MUST equal the sum of value fields across ALL steps (both required:true and required:false), treating omitted step value fields as 0. The Registry MUST enforce this constraint at submission time. MUST be present together with currency.

currency.description: OPTIONAL. ISO 4217 currency code for total_value and per-step value fields. MUST be present together with total_value. All step-level currency values MUST match this field when total_value is present.

notification_uri.description: OPTIONAL. HTTPS URI for principal notifications. When present, the Registry MUST POST a signed notification payload to this URI after transitioning the envelope to pending_approval status. The notification payload MUST include approval_id, description, principal_id, and a review_uri linking to the principal's approval consent UI.

creator_signature.description: Base64url-encoded EdDSA signature by the created_by agent's key over the JCS-canonical (RFC 8785) ApprovalEnvelopeSubmission with creator_signature set to empty string ''. The signing input includes creator_kid, engagement_id, and notification_uri when present. The canonical field order for signing is: approval_id, created_by, creator_kid, principal_id, engagement_id, description, approval_window_expires_at, created_at, notification_uri, steps, compensation_steps, total_value, currency, creator_signature.

status.description: READ-ONLY. Set by the Registry. Valid transitions are defined by the Approval Envelope lifecycle. Submitted envelopes start at pending_approval. Implementations MUST NOT set this field on submission.

principal_signature.description: CONDITIONAL. Base64url-encoded EdDSA signature by a verification method controlled by principal_id over the JCS-canonical immutable approval signing input with principal_signature set to empty string ''. The signing input is the accepted submission content plus creator_signature, principal_kid, and principal_signature, includes engagement_id and notification_uri when present, and excludes Registry-managed status, approved_at, and step status/timestamp fields. Submitted by a Principal Wallet upon approval and stored by the Registry through an atomic pending_approval -> approved transition. Absent on initial submission. Once stored, MUST NOT be replaced by later racing approval requests.

principal_kid.description: CONDITIONAL. DID URL identifying the Ed25519 verification method used to verify principal_signature. The DID portion MUST equal principal_id. Required whenever principal_signature is present and absent on initial submission.

approved_at.description: READ-ONLY. ISO 8601 UTC timestamp set by the Registry when the winning approval transition is committed. MUST NOT be replaced by later racing approval requests.

approval_envelope_submission.description: Profile for POST /v1/approvals request bodies. Read-only Registry-managed fields are forbidden.

stored_approval_envelope.description: Profile for Registry-stored and Registry-returned Approval Envelopes. Registry-managed lifecycle fields are present in read responses.

step.title: Approval Step
step.description: One atomic action at one Relying Party within the approval workflow.

step_index.description: 1-based unique step identifier within this envelope. MUST be sequential and unique. Referenced by triggered_by fields of dependent steps.

actor.description: AID of the agent that will execute this step. The Registry issues a Step Execution Token to this agent when the step is claimable.

relying_party_uri.description: URI of the Relying Party at which this step is executed. Included in the Step Execution Token so the RP can verify it is the intended recipient.

action_type.description: Application-defined action identifier describing the type of operation (e.g., 'payment.debit', 'order.place'). Displayed to the principal and included in the action_hash computation.

action_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical action descriptor, prefixed with 'sha256:'. Computed as SHA-256 of RFC 8785 JCS of {action_type, relying_party_uri, step_index, actor, <application-specific params>}. Binds the step to a specific operation — the Registry verifies this hash at claim time.

scopes.description: AIP scope strings required to claim and execute this step. The Credential Token used to claim the step MUST contain every scope in this set, and the Registry-issued Step Execution Token aip_scope MUST be exactly this set.

description.description: Plain-language description of this step. Displayed verbatim to the principal during the approval ceremony.

required.description: When false, this step is optional — the envelope can complete without it. When true, the step MUST be completed or the envelope transitions to failed/compensating. Required and optional steps both count toward total_value.

oneOf.description: null for root steps — claimable as soon as the envelope is in 'approved' status.

step_index of the prerequisite step. MUST reference an existing step_index within this envelope. A value of 0 is explicitly forbidden. This step may only be claimed after the referenced step has status 'completed'. Multiple steps MAY share the same triggered_by value, enabling parallel execution.

triggered_by.description: Dependency pointer implementing the SAGA DAG. null = no prerequisite (root step). Positive integer = step_index of prerequisite. Circular dependencies are rejected at submission with approval_envelope_invalid.

value.description: OPTIONAL. Financial value of this step in the envelope's currency. Summed across all steps (required and optional) to compute and validate total_value; omitted value fields contribute 0 to that sum.

currency.description: OPTIONAL. ISO 4217 currency code for this step's value. MUST match envelope-level currency when total_value is present.

compensation_index.description: OPTIONAL. 1-based identifier of the single compensation action for this forward step. Null or absent if no compensation is defined for this step. Non-null values MUST equal the compensation_index of exactly one entry in compensation_steps, and no two forward steps in the same envelope may reference the same compensation_index.

status.description: READ-ONLY. Set by the Registry. pending: waiting for prerequisite or approval. claimed: Step Execution Token issued. completed: Relying Party confirmed success. failed: execution failed. compensated: compensation step executed. skipped: optional step bypassed. cancelled: terminal cancellation due to parent Engagement Object termination.

claimed_at.description: READ-ONLY. ISO 8601 UTC timestamp set by the Registry when the Step Execution Token is issued.

completed_at.description: READ-ONLY. ISO 8601 UTC timestamp set by the Registry when the step is marked completed.

compensation_step.title: Compensation Step
compensation_step.description: A SAGA rollback action pre-authorised by the principal. Executed without requiring new human approval if a forward step fails after earlier steps completed.

compensation_index.description: 1-based identifier for this compensation step within compensation_steps. Values MUST be unique and sequential starting at 1. Referenced by steps[].compensation_index.

actor.description: AID of the agent that will execute the compensation/rollback action.

relying_party_uri.description: URI of the Relying Party at which the compensation is executed.

action_type.description: Application-defined identifier for the rollback operation (e.g., 'payment.refund', 'order.cancel').

action_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical compensation action descriptor. Computation is identical to forward step action_hash.

scopes.description: AIP scope strings required to claim and execute this compensation step. The Credential Token used to claim the compensation step MUST contain every scope in this set, and the Registry-issued Step Execution Token aip_scope MUST be exactly this set.

description.description: Plain-language description of the rollback action. Displayed to the principal during the initial approval ceremony — compensation steps receive the same consent weight as forward steps.

status.description: READ-ONLY. Set by the Registry when the compensation action is triggered.
